Styles › Colonial Revival

1880 – 1955 · USA

Colonial Revival

Refined symmetry recalling the colonial Georgian house.

  • Overview
  • Design Philosophy
  • Visual Characteristics
  • Materials
  • Color Palette
  • Façade Guide
  • Lighting
  • Building Types
  • Design Checklist
  • Common Mistakes
  • AI Prompts
  • Gallery
  • Related

01 — Overview

Overview

Definition. Colonial Revival reinterprets early American Georgian and Federal homes — symmetrical brick or clapboard façades, a centred pedimented doorway, multipane windows and restrained classical trim.

Historical background. A patriotic late-19th-century revival sparked by the 1876 Centennial, it became the enduring template for the dignified, symmetrical American house.

Origin. United States, 1880–1955.

Timeline

1876

Centennial sparks colonial nostalgia

1900

Colonial Revival becomes dominant

1930

Williamsburg restoration popularises it

1950

Suburban standard

Key Architects

McKim, Mead & WhiteRoyal Barry WillsVarious residential architects

Influential Projects

Colonial Revival mansion

Colonial Revival mansion

McKim, Mead & White · 1890
USA

Symmetrical brick Georgian-revival house

Symmetrical brick Georgian-revival house

— · 1915
USA

Clapboard Federal-revival house

Clapboard Federal-revival house

— · 1925
USA

02 — Design Philosophy

Design Philosophy

Core Principles

Symmetry & balanceClassical restraintTraditional materialsRefined proportionDomestic dignity

The Thinking

The exterior is calm and balanced — a symmetrical façade centred on a refined pedimented entrance, with multipane windows and modest classical trim conveying quiet dignity.

Values It Represents

TraditionDignityComfortRefinement

Why It Emerged

A patriotic, nostalgic return to America's colonial roots — a dignified, symmetrical, comfortable house for the new century.

03 — Visual Characteristics

Visual Characteristics

MassingSymmetrical rectangular blocks, often with side wings.
Form LanguageClean rectilinear forms with a centred entrance.
CompositionStrict bilateral symmetry about the front door.
ProportionBalanced, restrained classical proportion.
GeometryOrthogonal with classical accents.
RhythmEvenly-spaced regular windows.
SymmetryStrong bilateral symmetry.
ScaleDomestic and dignified.
Roof TypesSide-gabled or hipped roofs with dormers; modest cornice.
Window ProportionMultipane double-hung sash windows with shutters, symmetrically placed.
EntranceCentred pedimented doorway with pilasters or portico.
Façade ArticulationSymmetrical brick or clapboard with a pedimented door, multipane sash and restrained classical trim.

04 — Materials

Materials

Red brick

Robust, modular and warm — the everyday material of solid walls.

White clapboard

Traditional painted timber siding for crisp domestic façades.

White trim

Painted classical trim that frames and refines the façade.

Black shutters

Operable shutters that shade openings and order the façade.

Slate roof

Durable dark roofing that crowns the silhouette.

05 — Color Palette

Color Palette

Crisp, traditional and refined — white and brick with dark-shutter accents.

Primary

Colonial White
#f1ede4
Brick Red
#9a5a44

Accent

Federal Blue
#2f4a6a
Black
#2c2b29
Sage
#7a8a6a

Material Colors

Slate
#4f5358
Stone
#cabfaf

Typical Combinations

  • +Red brick + white trim + black shutters
  • +White clapboard + black shutters

06 — Façade Design Guide

Façade Design Guide

Window CompositionSymmetrical multipane double-hung sash windows with shutters.
EmphasisBalanced and symmetrical about the centred entrance.
Shading DevicesShutters, modest cornices and door porticoes.
BalconiesModest porches and Palladian-window accents.
FramesWhite-painted classical surrounds and shutters.
OpeningsEvenly-spaced, symmetrical, multipane openings.
Architectural DetailsPedimented doorway, dentil cornice, pilasters, fanlight.
OrnamentationRestrained and classical — a pedimented door and modest trim, never excessive.

07 — Lighting

Lighting

Natural Lighting Strategy

Symmetrical openings give even, balanced daylight; modest shadow from trim.

Artificial Lighting Concept

Warm, even; lit entrance portico and lantern.

Night Façade Appearance

Warm glow from symmetrical windows; lit centred doorway.

Recommendations

Warm 2700K; light the entrance portico; keep it even and calm.

08 — Typical Building Types

Typical Building Types

Where this style performs best:

HousesTown halls & librariesSchools & collegesInns & clubsEmbassiesCivic buildings

09 — Design Checklist

Design Checklist

Tap an item to mark it complete — saved to your browser.

  • Compose a strictly symmetrical façade
  • Centre a pedimented entrance
  • Use multipane sash windows with shutters
  • Use red brick or white clapboard
  • Add a dentil cornice and modest trim
  • Keep classical detail restrained
  • Use side-gabled or hipped roofs with dormers
  • Balance every opening about the centre

10 — Common Mistakes

Common Mistakes

Elements that contradict the style — what to avoid:

  • ✕Asymmetry breaking the balance
  • ✕Heavy or excessive ornament
  • ✕Large undivided modern glazing
  • ✕Mismatched proportions
  • ✕Bold non-traditional colours

11 — AI Prompt Examples

AI Prompt Examples

Optimised for Midjourney, ChatGPT image tools or Claude — exterior renders. Tap copy to use.

Exterior

Colonial Revival house exterior, symmetrical red-brick façade, centred white pedimented doorway, multipane sash windows with black shutters, side-gabled roof, dignified, architectural photography --ar 3:2 --style raw

Façade Concept

Colonial Revival façade, symmetrical elevation, pedimented entrance with pilasters, multipane sash, shutters, dentil cornice, frontal elevation, refined light

Night View

Colonial Revival house exterior at night, warm glow from symmetrical windows, lit centred portico, dignified, architectural night photography

Aerial View

Aerial of a Colonial Revival house, symmetrical block with wings, hipped roof and dormers, formal garden, drone view

Street Perspective

Street view of a Colonial Revival home exterior, brick façade, white pedimented doorway, shutters, mature trees, documentary photography

Masterplan

Colonial Revival neighbourhood masterplan, symmetrical houses along tree-lined streets, formal gardens, axonometric, brick-and-white palette
